meta.title: GAEA agentic intelligence briefs & forecast ledger
meta.note: The brief below is a SAMPLE demonstrating the agent pipeline's output format — structure, sourcing discipline, and attestation. Production briefs will be generated by the GAEA agent stack against live feeds, and every published brief and forecast is hashed for tamper-evidence.

briefs.title: Arctic Energy Weekly — output format specimen

sections.heading: Executive read
sections.body: This specimen shows the fixed structure of a GAEA weekly brief: an executive read of no more than four sentences, a supply-side section keyed to physical flows, an Arctic-specific section (the coverage no generalist desk maintains), and a markets-structure section keyed to the curve. Every factual claim in a production brief carries an inline source tag, and the full brief is hashed and attested via the GAEA oracle before publication.

sections.heading: Supply & flows
sections.body: Production briefs summarize the week's physical signal: OPEC+ policy statements, EIA and API inventory prints, rig counts, refinery outages, and tanker-flow anomalies detected from AIS data. Each item is tagged with its source and timestamp. The agent flags divergences between reported and observed flows as its primary alpha signal.

sections.heading: Arctic watch
sections.body: The Arctic section tracks what generalist coverage misses: Northern Sea Route transit activity and ice-season windows, Barents Sea development milestones, Alaska North Slope project status, sanctions pressure on Russian Arctic LNG, and Greenland licensing and policy signals — including any movement on the 2021 exploration moratorium.

sections.heading: Market structure
sections.body: The final section reads the futures curve rather than predicting price: shape (contango or backwardation), calendar-spread moves, crack-spread direction, and positioning data. GAEA briefs are informational market intelligence; they never contain trade recommendations or price targets presented as advice.

forecasts.question: Will any G7-jurisdiction issuer launch a tokenized crude oil or oil-royalty product with published proof-of-reserves by end of 2026?
forecasts.note: Specimen forecast demonstrating the ledger format

forecasts.question: Will Northern Sea Route full-year transit cargo volume exceed the prior year's total?
forecasts.note: Specimen forecast demonstrating the ledger format

forecasts.question: Will Greenland's government open a formal consultation on revisiting the 2021 hydrocarbon exploration moratorium within 12 months?
forecasts.note: Specimen forecast demonstrating the ledger format
